questionText:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:
The exercise was $\_\_\_\_$ difficult $\_\_\_\_$ nobody could finish it on time.
options: such / that
so / that
such / so
so / as
explanation: La structure correcte est 'so + adjectif + that' pour exprimer la conséquence. 'So' modifie l'adjectif 'difficult', et 'that' introduit la proposition de conséquence.

questionText:
Complete the sentence:
It was $\_\_\_\_$ a boring film $\_\_\_\_$ I fell asleep after 20 minutes.
options: so / that
such / that
so / as
such / so
explanation: On utilise 'such + (adjectif) + nom + that' pour exprimer la conséquence. Ici, 'film' est un nom singulier, donc 'such a boring film that'.

options: It was such a hot day that we stayed inside.
The weather was so hot that we stayed inside.
There were such many people that we couldn't move.
The music was so loud that I got a headache.
explanation: L'option C est incorrecte. 'Such' ne peut pas être utilisé directement avec 'many' ou 'much'. La forme correcte est 'so many/much + that'. On dirait: 'There were so many people that we couldn't move.'

questionText:
Combine the two sentences using 'so...that':
He speaks quickly. Nobody understands him.
options: He speaks such quickly that nobody understands him.
He so speaks quickly that nobody understands him.
He speaks so quickly that nobody understands him.
He speaks so quickly for nobody to understand him.
explanation: La structure correcte est 'so + adverbe + that'. 'Quickly' est un adverbe de manière, donc 'so quickly' est approprié, suivi de 'that' pour introduire la conséquence.

questionText: Look at the image and choose the correct sentence.
options: It is such a tall building that it touches the clouds.
It is so a tall building that it touches the clouds.
The building is such tall that it touches the clouds.
The building is so tall as it touches the clouds.
explanation: La phrase correcte utilise 'such a + adjectif + nom + that' (It is such a tall building that...). L'option C serait correcte si elle disait 'The building is so tall that...', mais elle utilise 'such tall' incorrectement.

questionText: He has $\_\_\_\_$ money $\_\_\_\_$ he can buy anything he wants.
options: such a / that
so much / that
such many / that
so little / as
explanation: Avec 'money' (nom indénombrable), on utilise 'so much' (et non 'such') suivi de 'that'. 'So much money that' exprime une grande quantité menant à une conséquence.

questionText:
Identify the function of 'that' in this sentence:
'It was such a terrible experience that I will never forget it.'
options: Relative pronoun (object)
Demonstrative adjective
Conjunction of consequence
Relative pronoun (subject)
explanation: Dans les structures 'so...that' et 'such...that', 'that' est une conjonction de subordination qui introduit une proposition de conséquence ou de résultat. Il n'a pas de fonction grammaticale à l'intérieur de la proposition subordonnée.

questionText:
Rewrite the sentence using 'such...that':
'The concert was so noisy that our ears were ringing.'
options: It was such a noisy concert that our ears were ringing.
The concert was such noisy that our ears were ringing.
It was so a noisy concert that our ears were ringing.
The concert was such a noisy that our ears were ringing.
explanation: Pour transformer 'so + adjectif' en 'such...that', on doit souvent ajouter un nom. La structure est 'such + a/an + adjectif + nom + that'. 'Such a noisy concert' est la forme correcte.

questionText: He was $\_\_\_\_$ an honest man $\_\_\_\_$ everyone trusted him completely.
options: so / that
such / that
so / as
such / so
explanation: On utilise 'such a/an + adjectif + nom singulier + that'. 'Man' est un nom, donc 'such an honest man that' est correct. Attention à l'utilisation de 'an' devant 'honest' (h muet).

questionText:
Choose the option with the same meaning as:
'She was so beautiful that everyone stared at her.'
options: She was too beautiful for everyone to stare at her.
She was such a beauty that everyone stared at her.
She was beautiful enough for everyone to stare at her.
She was so beautiful for everyone staring at her.
explanation: La phrase transformée garde le sens de cause à effet. 'She was such a beauty' (avec 'beauty' comme nom) équivaut à 'so beautiful' (adjectif). Les autres options changent le sens (too...to = trop...pour; enough = assez).
